title: Insect bite skin reaction

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting rapid-onset local reaction, redness, itch, type and severity, possible infection, systemic reaction, tick context, and worsening or systemic illness only when assessed.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact assessment section supports documenting an insect bite or sting context, onset, local inflammatory or allergic symptoms, distribution, infection features and systemic-reaction assessment without inferring infection or allergy.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting rapid-onset local reaction, redness, itch, type and severity, possible infection, systemic reaction, tick context, and worsening or systemic illness only when assessed.

source.title: Insect bites and stings: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations

referral.text: Supports documenting infection development, rapid worsening, systemic illness, severe disproportionate pain, previous systemic reaction, immunocompromise, mouth, throat or eye location, exotic exposure, fever, persistent lesions, and travel context.
referral.rationale: The exact reassessment and referral qualifiers support recording severe pain, immunocompromise, worsening, systemic illness, previous systemic reaction, sensitive-site, exotic-insect, fever and travel context only when assessed, plus clinician-discussed safety net and arranged review.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting infection development, rapid worsening, systemic illness, severe disproportionate pain, previous systemic reaction, immunocompromise, mouth, throat or eye location, exotic exposure, fever, persistent lesions, and travel context.

source.title: Insect bites and stings: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.8–1.1.11 — reassessment, referral, and specialist-advice qualifiers

source_limitations: Exact insect or arthropod, bite time and place, travel destination, lesion morphology and dimensions, allergy history, anaphylaxis criteria, tick and Lyme assessment, infection diagnosis, treatment, medicine, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
